title: Interpret An AGV Departure Without A Replacement AGV Behind It
summary: Use the training-source explanation of AGV exchange behavior to interpret a visible gap when a front AGV moves out without another AGV replacing it behind. The documented meaning is that normal exchange behavior is paired, so a gap likely indicates the trailing AGV faulted or was not backfilled properly.
when_to_use: Use when a user or support person observes a front AGV move out and no replacement AGV appears behind it, and the goal is to interpret that condition using the training source's documented AGV exchange behavior.
not_for: Do not use to infer causes beyond the source-described explanations of trailing AGV fault or improper backfill.
Do not use as a repair or recovery procedure; this runbook only supports interpretation of the observed condition.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe or review the AGV exchange condition, compare it to the documented paired-command behavior, and record the source-supported interpretation.

safety_notes: This is a reference/interpretation procedure only and does not direct physical intervention.
Do not infer additional causes beyond the source-described fault or improper backfill explanation.
access_or_tools_needed: Visual access to the AGV station or aisle

access_required: Visual access to the AGV station or aisle
system_preconditions: A front AGV departure without a visible replacement AGV behind it has been observed or reported.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1 segment describing AGV exchange pairing and backfill exception

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript segment describing the abnormal case where an AGV moves out without another replacing it, and explaining that the trailing AGV likely faulted or was not backfilled properly.
Training video frame describing normal AGV tote exchange behavior, including simultaneous move-out and move-in during exchange.

steps.title: Confirm the visible AGV gap condition
steps.purpose: Establish that the observed condition matches the scenario addressed by the source.
steps.instruction: Observe the station or aisle and confirm that a front AGV moved out without another AGV replacing it behind.
steps.expected_result: The observed condition is clearly identified as a front AGV departure with no visible replacement AGV behind it.
steps.healthy_condition: A replacement AGV is present behind the departing front AGV as part of normal exchange behavior.
steps.failure_condition: A front AGV has moved out and no replacement AGV is behind it.

actions.label: Observe AGV position change
actions.exact_action: Observe the station or aisle and confirm that a front AGV moved out without another AGV replacing it behind.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The training frame and transcript describing the case where an AGV moves out without another one replacing it.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed movement does not clearly match the documented scenario of a front AGV moving out without replacement.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If an AGV moves out without replacement, the AGV behind it likely faulted or was not backfilled properly.

steps.title: Compare the observation to normal paired exchange behavior
steps.purpose: Use the source-defined normal behavior as the baseline for interpretation.
steps.instruction: Use the documented exchange behavior as the comparison point: the front AGV will not move until there is an AGV behind it because software sends both commands at the same time.
steps.expected_result: The observer understands that normal exchange behavior is paired and should include a trailing AGV behind the front AGV before move-out.
steps.healthy_condition: The front AGV does not move until the AGV behind it is present, consistent with paired software commands.
steps.failure_condition: The observed gap does not match normal paired exchange behavior.

actions.label: Review paired exchange behavior
actions.exact_action: Use the documented exchange behavior as the comparison point: the front AGV will not move until there is an AGV behind it because software sends both commands at the same time.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The normal exchange sequence showing the AGV with the full tote moving out while the AGV with the empty tote moves into the rack location.
The explanation that the front AGV will not move until there is an AGV behind it because software sends both commands at the same time.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed AGV movement does not match the documented paired-command behavior and no source-supported interpretation can be applied.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The front AGV will not move until AGV behind it; software sends both commands at the same time.

steps.title: Check for the source-described exception condition
steps.purpose: Determine whether the visible gap matches the source-described abnormal case.
steps.instruction: Check whether the trailing AGV has a fault or whether the situation appears to match the source-described case where the AGV behind was reported ready for a new task while also faulting, resulting in improper backfill.
steps.expected_result: The observed gap is associated with one of the source-supported exception explanations: trailing AGV fault or improper backfill.
steps.healthy_condition: No gap is present because the trailing AGV is available and backfill occurs normally.
steps.failure_condition: The trailing AGV appears faulted or the exchange appears improperly backfilled, leaving a visible gap.

actions.label: Assess trailing AGV exception
actions.exact_action: Check whether the trailing AGV has a fault or whether the situation appears to match the source-described case where the AGV behind was reported ready for a new task while also faulting, resulting in improper backfill.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The transcript text stating that a missing replacement can happen if the AGV behind faulted or was not backfilled properly.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if no trailing AGV fault or improper backfill explanation can be confirmed from available evidence.
Stop interpretation at the source-supported causes and do not infer additional causes.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If an AGV moves out without replacement, the AGV behind it likely faulted or was not backfilled properly.

source_evidence.summary: A missing replacement can occur if the trailing AGV faults or is reported ready for a new task while also faulting, causing improper backfill.

steps.title: Record the source-supported interpretation
steps.purpose: Document the observed condition using the source-backed explanation and avoid unsupported conclusions.
steps.instruction: Record that the observed gap is not normal paired exchange behavior and matches the source-described fault or backfill exception.
steps.expected_result: The condition is documented as an abnormal exchange gap consistent with trailing AGV fault or improper backfill.
steps.healthy_condition: Documentation reflects that normal paired exchange behavior would include a replacement AGV behind the departing AGV.
steps.failure_condition: Documentation treats the gap as normal behavior or assigns unsupported causes.

actions.label: Document interpretation
actions.exact_action: Record that the observed gap is not normal paired exchange behavior and matches the source-described fault or backfill exception.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide or frame showing AGV exchange/backfill context behind a departing AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed AGV movement does not match the documented paired-command behavior and no trailing AGV fault or backfill issue can be confirmed from available evidence.
Do not record additional causes beyond the source-described fault or improper backfill explanation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The front AGV will not move until AGV behind it; software sends both commands at the same time. If an AGV moves out without replacement, the AGV behind it likely faulted or was not backfilled properly.

success_criteria: The user can interpret the visible AGV gap as a likely trailing AGV fault or improper backfill condition rather than normal exchange behavior.
The documented interpretation remains limited to the source-supported explanation.
healthy_conditions: Normal exchange behavior is paired, with the front AGV not moving until an AGV is behind it.
A replacement AGV is present behind the departing AGV during normal exchange.
failure_conditions: A front AGV moves out without another AGV replacing it behind.
The trailing AGV appears faulted or the exchange appears improperly backfilled.
The observed condition cannot be matched to the documented paired-command behavior using available evidence.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the observed AGV movement does not match the documented paired-command behavior and no trailing AGV fault or backfill issue can be confirmed from available evidence.
Do not infer additional causes beyond the source-described fault or improper backfill explanation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Abnormal AGV exchange/backfill context and transcript describing the missing replacement case.
Normal AGV exchange sequence for comparison.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The front AGV will not move until AGV behind it; software sends both commands at the same time. If an AGV moves out without replacement, the AGV behind it likely faulted or was not backfilled properly.
